segs.utf8:
I'm Nicole Delacroix.
I'm Allie's personal assistant.
I'll just tell Allie, you're here.
You're from San Francisco, aren't you?

Allie, it's good to see you again.
How are you?
Did you have a good journey?
Let me introduce you to the team.

You've met Nicole, my
personal assistant.

This is Jacques Lemaitre,
our PR director.
How do you do, Mark Ryder?
And this is Ben, our designer.

- Hi Mark.
- Great to meet you Ben.
We've heard a lot about you.
Ok. Should we go to my office?

Paris is so romantic.
I can't believe we're
here together at last.
It's wonderful. I really missed you.
So did you like the office?
How do you get out with everyone?

Ok, but we'll see.
I've only been here 3 weeks.
What did you think of them?
Oh, I thought shock was very nice.
You know we have to keep things secret.

I don't want the people in the
office to know we're together.
But it isn't going to be easy.

But it's not like I'm having my
own place I have to find an apartment.
Don't worry, won't take you long.
Do you really want to know?

I was wondering what
kind of boss you'll be.

Mark, would you mind sending
me those concept dates?

Is it Ok if I take
tomorrow afternoon off?
I'm sorry, but tomorrow is really difficult.

Do you think you could send
me the request by email?
Could you hold a moment, Mark?

Can you come and see me
when you have a moment?

Have you started
looking for an apartment?
No, I haven't had time yet.
No, it's best to get to know Paris first.

- Yeah, it's a big city.
-  Yeah
That's nearly all the French I know.
How do you like the office?

Really friendly. I like Ben a lot.
He's amazing with computers.

Jacques is very charming.
Everybody likes him and
he has a lovely wife.

She used to be a pop
star when she was young.
Have you heard of Elisabeth?
Although her clothes are very English.

You know today I asked her
if I could have a day off.

And she wanted me
to send her an email.
Well, the English have their funny ways.

Allie! Hi!
I get you a drink.
Thanks. I'll have a diet Coke.

I'm sorry.
I didn't catch that.
I think I found an apartment.
How do I get to Belleville?

The easiest way is to get
the metro at Pyramides.
Take line 14 and change at Châtelet.

Then take line 11 towards
Mairie des Lilas.
And how many stops is it?

Oh, right.
I found it on the map.
How long does it take to get there?
When are you going to see it?

If you can wait till six,
I'll give you a lift.

I live near Belleville.
So I'm driving that way.
Sorry Nicole, what did she say?

She said we can have a look at
the flat. She's going to wait downstairs.
It's a long way from the station.
It's a pity there isn't a lift.

Who needs one the
stairs are good exercise?
Look, there's a great view from here.

It's just how I imagined
an apartment in Paris.

Including the heating
it would be very cold in winter.
Are you going to take it?
I can't wait to see it.

No, I'm with the woman
who owns the apartment.
I'll call you back.
You know, she's just taking an interest.

I got a message this
morning, it's from Jacques.

I'm in Rome, my return
flight's been cancelled.
There's a small problem.

Scarlett Scarpino is in Paris for
her concerts of this evening.
I was going to look after her today.
Could you possibly take care of her?

You've met Scarlett
Scarpino, haven't you, Ben?

The punk princess.
Yeah, I met her in London last year.
Let's say she's a bit difficult.
What are we going to do with her?
Why don't you show her around Paris?
Why don't you show her around Paris?
You can't leave me to do this on my own.
Ok, why don't we take her to Nootre Dame?
I mean, it's her first time in Paris, isn't it?
I don't think churches are really her thing.
How about taking her on a boat trip?
and then we can go up the Eiffel Tower.

That's a good idea.
I'm sure she'll love the view.
Shall we have lunch after that?
Do you have any recommendations, Ben?
Ben, do you want to come, too?

But I'm sure you'll have
an unforgettable meal.
Sure but this food's really horrible.

This is one of the finest
restaurants in Paris.

Well, I can't eat this stuff.
I never touch meat.

Didn't they tell you guys
about my allergies?

I'm allergic to mushrooms,
strawberries, nuts...

Whatever.
I'm going to the restroom.
Well, that was a disastrous this morning.

The boat trip made her feel sick
and she wouldn't go up the Eiffel Tower.

It's a pity we didn't just
take her shopping.
Oh, come on. She's just a kid really.
So, what are we going to do about lunch?

No, hang on. I have an idea.
Let me talk to the waiter.

Do you think you could
possibly do me a favor?
Yes, of course, sir. What would you like?
Well, I think this place is great.

- That was a great concert last night, Scarlett .
- Thanks.

As we know Scarlett's got
a new CD coming out soon.

So let's have a look at the best way.
We can promote it in France.

Well, I think Scarlett should visit the
major music stores.

In my opinion, that's the
best way to meet her fans.

I'm not so sure.
What do you think, Jacques?
Actually, I don't agree with Mark.
Scarlett isn't commercial in that way.

I don't have a commercial image.
It isn't my style.
Ok, but Scarlett needs more publicity.

What about a series of TV
and radio interviews?
What we want is something different.

Personally, I think Scarlett should
tour clubs and summer festivals.
She can DJ, play her favorite music...
...play the new CD and meet her fans, too.
Yes, absolutely. That's a much better idea.
It's great to be on our own again.

Is this the first time you've
been to the Louvre?

Because I agree with
Jacques and not with you?
Yeah, well, we knew it wouldn't be easy.
It's difficult for me as well.
But if I don't agree with you.
I know, I know, you're the boss.
And I have to do my job.

I really thought that Jacques' idea
was better. And so did Scarlett.

It's not a big deal, Allie.
I'm fine, really.
So who exactly was the Mona Lisa?

I'm not sure.
I think she was the wife of a banker.

Is that why she's smiling?
Because her husband has a good salary?

I also read somewhere that
she was a self-portrait of Leonardo.

Now, I don't know much about art...
...but Leonardo da Vinci was a man, right?
Why do you think she's smiling?

Well, in my opinion, she's the
managing director of a music company.

She lives in Paris, she's in love with
her marketing director... and she has
a lot of fun telling him what to do.

Hey, we're not in the office now.
You can't tell me I'm wrong.
I've just seen Ben from the office.

I don't think he's seen us.
Let's get out of here. Come on!
Did you have a nice weekend?

Oh, yeah,
You'll never guess who I saw on Saturday.
Allie and Mark in the Louvre together.

And they looked really close.
I think they were holding hands.

And I think they saw me because
they turned and left really quickly.

I think they've been seeing each
other ever since Mark arrived.

When I went to look at Mark's new
apartment. I'm sure Allie called
him on his mobile.

And I've seen her looking at him ...
in certain way...

Please find attached a copy of
the latest sales report from the USA.
So, did you guys have a good weekend?
Oh, I spent most of the time at home.

Just being domestic, you know.
The apartment's looking pretty nice now.

You must come around
for a meal one evening.
So didn't you go out at all?

Oh,
Sure, I went to the Louvre on Saturday.
I felt like getting a bit of culture.

I kind of prefer going to
museums and galleries on my own.
You can look at everything at your own pace.
I went to the Louvre on Saturday, too.
I've just had an email from Allie.

Dear Mark,
Thank you for the information.

And thank you, darling, for a wonderful weekend.
Allie.

I think there's something more
important to talk about right now.

You hit 'reply to all'. You sent
it to everyone in the office.
It's an easy mistake to make.
How could I be so stupid?
Don't worry about it. It doesn't matter.
But I think we should talk to the others.
I'll do it. It was my fault.
I just want to say that I'm terribly sorry.
I haven't been honest with you.

That's Ok, Allie.
We had already guessed.
It wasn't really a surprise.
I still can't work out how they knew about us.

I was always really careful
not to treat you differently.
You were really hard on me.
Oh, you were just being fair and very British.
So if it wasn't me, it must have been you.

I've got my own office. You're
with them all the time.

You must have said something.
You're hopeless at keeping secrets!

Don't blame me.
This wasn't my fault.
They probably just guessed.

You know the French, they're
experts on love affairs.

Actually, I think it's great that
everyone knows. Now we don't
have to pretend any more.

Allie, there's something I've been
wanting to ask you for a long time.

Sorry Mark,
I didn't hear a word you said.

Can you confirm that in an email
for me? Just don't send it to
everyone in the office this time.
